workoutInstruction:
Welcome to Week 9 in this training block.
LET'S GO!

“Body Armor”

Complete the following for time:
Run 1 Mile
100 Pull Ups
200 Push Ups
300 Squats
Run 1 Mile

**If you have body armor or a weight vest, wear it

exercises.groupInstruction:
In memory of Navy Lieutenant Michael Murphy, 29, of Patchogue, NY, who was killed in Afghanistan June 28th, 2005.
This workout was one of Mike's favorites and he'd named it "Body Armor" From here on it will be referred to as "Murph" in honor of the focused warrior and great American who wanted nothing more in life than to serve this great country and the beautiful people who make it what it is. First posted 18 August 2005.

For time:
1mile Run
100 Pull-Ups
200 Push-Ups
300 Air Squats
1mile Run

Partition the pull-ups, push-ups, and squats as needed. Start and finish with a mile run. If you have a twenty pound vest or body armor, wear it.

title: BB 7.1
workoutInstruction:
Welcome to Week 12 in this training block.

This week is EITHER:
- Testing
OR
- De-Load
(for our main lifts)

Why?

1.  Some of you will be feeling fatigued & may need a de-load (some of you will not)
2.  Some of the women may be hitting the week of their cycle where hormonally, you're at your weakest (some of you will not)
3.  Some days you have peak energy, drive, arousal & motivation.. some days you don't

I wanted to give an opportunity over a 2 week basis to de-load when you need it, AND, test when you're feeling up to it.

Any questions - hit me up & let me know.


LET'S GO!

Find a 1 rep max back squat...

OR...

De-load:  3 sets of 5, at 50-60% of your top weight from last week

title: BB 7.1
workoutInstruction:
Welcome to Week 1 in this training block.

This block has a Functional Bodybuilding focus I'm really excited to bring you!

We will be working on:
- Building lean muscle mass
- Muscle hypertrophy
- Working through greater ranges of motion
- Taking care of your muscles, ligaments & joints for longevity
- Aesthetics - arms, booty, abs

In terms of programming for intensity, we will be using RPE (Rate of Perceived Exertion), rather than programming specific %'s of your 1RM.  As you get stronger, those %'s change throughout the block, so they end up being inaccurate & they don't take into account how you feel on the day.  RPE uses a scale between 1-10 to have a subjective measure for how hard you feel you're working.  For Bodybuilding, most of the time, we want that to be around a RPE 8 or higher!  We need to put your muscles to work so they can adapt ;)

Reminder:  make sure you are getting 7+ hours of sleep, drinking 2-3L of water daily & are eating enough calories for your body (a good measure is also 1g protein per lb of lean mass you have).

Any questions, let me know!  Excited.

LET'S GO!

exercises.instruction: If you can, find a weight that feels like a RPE 8.5-9 on every set today

workoutInstruction:
Welcome to Week 1 in this training block.
LET'S GOOOOOO!
____________________________

We're doing a FRENCH CONTRAST Squat Training Block leading into testing day in November!
Format:

3 sets:
Heavy Compound (Strength) 80–85% 1RM
Plyometric (Bodyweight explosive)
Light Compound (Speed strength) ~30% 1RM
Assisted Plyometric (overspeed)

Rest: :20-30 between movements
& 2–3 min between rounds

Load: Conservative for this week - focus on speed & control

___________________________

Back Squat – 3 reps @ ~80–85% 1RM
Jump Squats (bodyweight) – 3 reps (focus on max height + speed)
Back Squat (30% 1RM or loaded bar jump squats) – 3 reps (fast as possible)
Band-Assisted Jump Squats – 3 reps


Complete 3 Rounds for quality:
8 DB Eccentric Front-Foot Elevated Split Squat (:03ea)
6 Paused Cossack Squats (:01ea)
10 Lateral Sled Drag (ie. 10 steps each side)


Core Finisher:
5 Lateral Med Ball Slam (ea)
10 High Plank Drag (ea)
8 Weighted Deadbug (ea)

exercises.instruction: Jump Squats (bodyweight) – 3 reps (focus on max height + speed)
Back Squat (30% 1RM or loaded bar jump squats) – 3 reps (fast as possible)
8 DB Eccentric Front-Foot Elevated Split Squat (:03ea)
10 Lateral Sled Drag (ie. 10 steps each side)
